insight:
Looking at yesterday's data, you had a solid rest day with 6,987 steps and no formal training - perfect timing just 3 days out from Chester! Your resting heart rate of 44 bpm shows excellent fitness, and that low stress level of 30/100 indicates you're staying calm before race day. You burned 2,399 calories, which aligns well with your 2,300 target.

Your HRV at 118ms is actually above your baseline range of 93-116ms, which is fantastic news! This elevated reading suggests your body is recovering well and adapting positively to your training taper. Combined with your low stress and solid resting HR, you're primed to stick with whatever light session you had planned for today - no need to modify or skip anything.

With only 6.7 hours of sleep, prioritize getting 7-8 hours tonight. Your body is clearly ready to perform, but quality sleep over the next two nights will be crucial for maintaining that excellent HRV and ensuring you're sharp on race morning.
